implementationGroups.title: Architecture risk: broad target endpoints
requiredBehaviors: The HarmonyOS bootstrap route host performs the same page-entry initialization by calling renderHomeRoute with current route-owned search/filter state and a freshly loaded HomePageState whenever the home route becomes active.

The HarmonyOS route preserves the same observable behavior split: bootstrap-page @State fields update home search/filter immediately, and renderHomeRoute receives repository-backed HomePageState plus those live route-owned values on each re-render.

FoodikeBootstrap.buildHistoryRoute recreates the history route with a freshly loaded HistoryPageState from historyStore.loadHistoryState whenever the history route becomes active, then passes the route-owned search and tab state into renderHistoryRoute for...

HistoryStore.loadHistoryState exposes the shared favourites snapshot for renderHistoryRoute, and HistoryStore.selectRestaurant writes the selected restaurant before navigation while FoodikeBootstrap rerenders the history route from its route-owned tab/query...

FoodikeBootstrap owns the history route entry state through historyTabIndex and historySearchQuery, and buildHistoryRoute immediately loads HistoryPageState then renders the history route with that current route-owned state whenever the page becomes active.
riskHints: current evidence uses broad shell/root/whole-file endpoints
acceptanceEvidence: Replace broad endpoints with precise target functions, types, resources, or tests before closing the group.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: presentation/history
requiredBehaviors: HistoryViewModel exposes compose-observed HistoryState backed by getLikedRestaurants(), and SelectRestaurant mutates repository state before invoking the navigation callback so favourites rendering and selection stay in sync with shared session data.

Source review found no runtime permission request or protected API access in HistoryViewModel; it only collects liked restaurants into screen state and stores the selected restaurant before invoking the navigation callback.

HistoryViewModel initializes by collecting getLikedRestaurants() as soon as the screen-scoped ViewModel is created, so the history screen enters with the current liked-restaurant snapshot ready for rendering.
HistorySection renders static order-history cards with non-veg item icons alongside the order summary and status text.
Source review found no runtime permission request or protected API access in HistorySection; the composable only renders static order-history UI.
History composes a route-local search bar, tab strip, and tab content surface, and favourite-card selection dispatches SelectRestaurant before navigating to the restaurant detail route.

History observes the current liked-restaurants state from the ViewModel and passes that state plus pagerState into TabsContent, so tab content and favourites selection recompose from the current route state.
Source review found no runtime permission request or protected API access in History; it only composes the search bar, tab strip, and tab content surface.
History creates a remembered pager state, reads the current liked-restaurant snapshot from the ViewModel, and immediately composes the search, tabs, and current tab content when the route enters.
Source review found no intent, URI, bundle, or cross-screen parameter flow in Tabs; the composable only switches the local pager page between History and Favourites tabs.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: presentation/home
requiredBehaviors: Home shows image-backed discovery content across ads, recommended categories, favourite restaurants, and the main restaurant list.
HomeScreen sends the tapped restaurant through a cross-screen selection command that stores the restaurant in session state before navigating to the detail route.
HomeScreen exposes home-specific navigation actions through the top section profile entry and restaurant-card/favourite selection actions that transition into profile or detail flows.
HomeScreen observes view-model state for loaded ads, food items, liked restaurants, and restaurant list, while local screen state updates search/filter controls immediately in place.
Source review found no runtime permission request or protected API access in HomeScreen; the screen only composes repository-backed discovery sections and navigation callbacks.
Home enters with route-owned initialization that immediately composes discovery content and binds the current view-model state when the screen is shown.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: presentation/components
requiredBehaviors: RestaurantCard formats the rating-count badge through getCustomerInfo, preserving the source threshold ordering for customer-count display inside each card.
RestaurantCard renders the restaurant hero image and rating star icon as part of the list card presentation.
Source review found no runtime permission request or protected API access in RestaurantCard; the composable only formats and displays restaurant card content.
SearchBar presents a single-line search input with a visible "Search" placeholder and leading search icon, updating the current query text on every input change.
Source review found no bitmap, preview, gallery, EXIF, or zoom behavior in SearchBar; the composable only shows a search text field with a leading icon.
SearchBar owns a remembered text state and updates it immediately through onValueChange so the visible query text recomposes in place as the user types.
Source review found no runtime permission request, permission callback, or protected API access in SearchBar; the composable only owns a remembered search text field and leading icon.
Source review found no settings/preferences persistence, option toggles, or configuration state in SearchBar; the composable only remembers the current text field value for the visible search input.

sourceReviewRequired: re-read relevant source files before implementing or closing UI-related rows

check runtime state, permission/result callbacks, data refresh, and item-action flows that static analysis may miss

matchedGroups.title: Home discovery content and restaurant selection
requiredBehaviors: Home sections appear in the same source order.
A selected restaurant is saved before detail navigation occurs.
riskHints: Do not push home-specific state into the global shell.
Search and filter controls are visible behavior even without backend filtering.
acceptanceEvidence: Target tests verify selection command ordering.
Build gate passes for phase-04-home-history-discovery.
uiContracts: home top section routes to profile
restaurant tap stores selection then routes to detail

matchedGroups.title: History tabs and favourites reuse

riskHints: The source history section is largely placeholder content; keep visible structure without inventing backend behavior.
acceptanceEvidence: Target history store test proves selection command behavior.
Page build shows tab sections with the expected labels and ordering.
uiContracts: history page shows search bar and tabs
restaurant tap stores selection then routes to detail
dataContracts: liked restaurants come from the same session repository as home

coordinationNotes: Prefer extending existing route/navigation/state surfaces over creating parallel shells.
If a shared surface must change, keep the change minimal and update only evidence rows tied to this group.
UI groups should preserve route exclusivity, back behavior, visible action feedback, and page-specific builders.
Repository/store groups should preserve existing data ownership and avoid duplicating global state.
